metadata.name: quickbooks__ap_ar_enhanced
metadata.comment:
Table that unions all accounts payable records from int_quickbooks__bill_join and all accounts receivable records from int_quickbooks__invoice_join while also adding customer, vendor, address, and department level detail to the accounts payable and receivable records. Further, date and amount
calculations are included to show if the payable/receivable has been fully paid and/or paid on time.

transaction_type.name: transaction_type
transaction_type.comment: Set to 'bill' of the transaction is a bill and 'invoice' if the transaction is an invoice.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.comment: Unique identifier of the transaction for the bill or invoice.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be null.

estimate_id.name: estimate_id
estimate_id.comment: Unique identifier of the estimate associated with the invoice.

department_name.name: department_name
department_name.comment: Name of the department associated with either the invoice or bill.

transaction_with.name: transaction_with
transaction_with.comment: Set to 'vendor' if the transaction type is a bill and 'customer' if the transaction type is an invoice.

customer_vendor_name.name: customer_vendor_name
customer_vendor_name.comment: Full name of the vendor (if a bill) or the customer (if an invoice).

customer_vendor_balance.name: customer_vendor_balance
customer_vendor_balance.comment: Total current account balance of the vendor (if a bill) or the customer (if an invoice).

customer_vendor_address_city.name: customer_vendor_address_city
customer_vendor_address_city.comment: City of the vendor (if a bill) or the customer (if an invoice).

customer_vendor_address_country.name: customer_vendor_address_country
customer_vendor_address_country.comment: Country of the vendor (if a bill) or the customer (if an invoice).

customer_vendor_address_line.name: customer_vendor_address_line
customer_vendor_address_line.comment: Address line of the vendor (if a bill) or the customer (if an invoice).

customer_vendor_website.name: customer_vendor_website
customer_vendor_website.comment: The website url of the customer or vendor.

delivery_type.name: delivery_type
delivery_type.comment: How the invoice was delivered to the customer.

estimate_status.name: estimate_status
estimate_status.comment: The status of the invoice estimate (if applicable).

total_amount.name: total_amount
total_amount.comment: The total monetary amount of the invoice or bill.

total_converted_amount.name: total_converted_amount
total_converted_amount.comment: The total monetary converted amount, with exchange rates applied if available, of the invoice or bill.

estimate_total_amount.name: estimate_total_amount
estimate_total_amount.comment: The total amount estimated of the bill or invoice (if applicable).

estimate_total_converted_amount.name: estimate_total_converted_amount
estimate_total_converted_amount.comment: The total converted amount, with exchange rates applied if available, estimated of the bill or invoice (if applicable).

current_balance.name: current_balance
current_balance.comment: The bill or invoice defined remaining balance of the bill or invoice.

due_date.name: due_date
due_date.comment: The due date for the bill or invoice.

is_overdue.name: is_overdue
is_overdue.comment: Boolean indicating whether the bill or invoice full payment is overdue.

days_overdue.name: days_overdue
days_overdue.comment: The recent payment minus the due date to show the total days the total payment is overdue.

initial_payment_date.name: initial_payment_date
initial_payment_date.comment: The first date a payment was provided for the bill or invoice

recent_payment_date.name: recent_payment_date
recent_payment_date.comment: The last date a payment was provided for the bill or invoice.

total_current_payment.name: total_current_payment
total_current_payment.comment: Sum of all total payments made towards the bill or invoice.

total_current_converted_payment.name: total_current_converted_payment
total_current_converted_payment.comment: Sum of all total converted payments, with exchange rates applied if available, made towards the bill or invoice.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: quickbooks__balance_sheet
metadata.comment: Table containing all asset, liability, and equity account classes by calendar year and month enriched with account type, class, and parent information.

calendar_date.name: calendar_date
calendar_date.comment: Timestamp of the first calendar date of the month. This is slated to be deprecated, and the fields `period_first_day` and `period_last_day` are both offered as replacements, depending on how your company performs their financial reporting.

period_first_day.name: period_first_day
period_first_day.comment: Timestamp of the first date of the month.

period_last_day.name: period_last_day
period_last_day.comment: Timestamp of the last date of the month.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be null.

account_class.name: account_class
account_class.comment: Class of the account associated with the balance sheet line.

class_id.name: class_id
class_id.comment: Reference to the class associated with the account.

is_sub_account.name: is_sub_account
is_sub_account.comment: Boolean indicating whether the account is a sub account (true) or a parent account (false).

parent_account_number.name: parent_account_number
parent_account_number.comment: The parent account number. If the account is the parent account then the account number is recorded.

parent_account_name.name: parent_account_name
parent_account_name.comment: The parent account name. If the account is the parent account then the account name is recorded.

account_type.name: account_type
account_type.comment: The type of account associated with the balance sheet line.

account_sub_type.name: account_sub_type
account_sub_type.comment: Sub type of the account associated with the balance sheet line.

account_number.name: account_number
account_number.comment: User defined number of the account.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The identifier of the account associated with the balance sheet line.

account_name.name: account_name
account_name.comment: Name of the account associated with the balance sheet line.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: The total ending period balance.

converted_amount.name: converted_amount
converted_amount.comment: The total ending period balance, converted with exchange rates applied if available,

account_ordinal.name: account_ordinal
account_ordinal.comment: Integer value to order the account within final financial statement reporting. The customer can also configure the ordinal; [see the README for details](https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_quickbooks/blob/main/README.md#customize-the-account-ordering-of-your-profit-loss-and-balance-sheet-models)

source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be null.

class_id.comment: Reference to the class associated with the account.

is_sub_account.comment: Boolean indicating whether the account is a sub account (true) or a parent account (false).

parent_account_number.name: parent_account_number
parent_account_number.comment: The parent account number. If the account is the parent account then the account number is recorded.

parent_account_name.name: parent_account_name
parent_account_name.comment: The parent account name. If the account is the parent account then the account name is recorded.

account_type.name: account_type
account_type.comment: The type of account associated with the cash flow line.

account_sub_type.name: account_sub_type
account_sub_type.comment: Sub type of the account associated with the cash flow line.

account_number.name: account_number
account_number.comment: User defined number of the account.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The identifier of the account associated with the cash flow line.

account_name.name: account_name
account_name.comment: Name of the account associated with the cash flow line.

cash_ending_period.comment: The total ending period balance cash for every cash flow account line.

cash_converted_ending_period.name: cash_converted_ending_period
cash_converted_ending_period.comment: The total ending period balance cash, converted with exchange rates applied if available, for every cash flow account line.

account_unique_id.name: account_unique_id
account_unique_id.comment: Unique identifier of the cash flow statement account line, dependent on account_id, class_id, source_relation and calendar_date.

cash_flow_type.name: cash_flow_type
cash_flow_type.comment: The type of cash flow (Defaults are Cash or Cash Equivalents, Investing, Operating, Financing). The customer can also configure the cash flow type; [see the README for details](https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_quickbooks/blob/main/README.md#customize-the-cash-flow-model).

cash_flow_ordinal.comment: Integer value to order the account within final cash flow statement reporting. The customer can also configure the ordinal; [see the README for details](https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_quickbooks/blob/main/README.md#customize-the-cash-flow-model).

cash_beginning_period.name: cash_beginning_period
cash_beginning_period.comment: The total beginning period balance cash for every cash flow account line.

cash_net_period.name: cash_net_period
cash_net_period.comment: The net cash change from the beginning to the end of the period for every cash flow account line.

cash_converted_beginning_period.name: cash_converted_beginning_period
cash_converted_beginning_period.comment: The total beginning period balance cash, converted with exchange rates applied if available, for every cash flow account line.

cash_converted_net_period.name: cash_converted_net_period
cash_converted_net_period.comment: The net cash change, converted with exchange rates applied if available, from the beginning to the end of the period for every cash flow account line.

account_number.comment: User defined number of the account. for the account associated with either the expense or sale.

department_id.comment: Reference to the department associated with either the expense or sale.

metadata.name: quickbooks__general_ledger
metadata.comment: Table that unions all records from each model within the double_entry_transactions directory. The table end result is a comprehensive general ledger with an offsetting debit and credit entry for each transaction.

unique_id.name: unique_id
unique_id.comment: Unique identifier of the general ledger line, dependent on `transaction_id`, `transaction_index`,  `account_id`, `transaction_type`, `transaction_source`, and `source_relation`.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.comment: Unique identifier of the transaction. Each transaction will have an offsetting debit and credit record.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source of the record if the unioning functionality is being used. If not this field will be null.

transaction_index.name: transaction_index
transaction_index.comment: Unique identifier of the individual debit and credit records of the transaction.

transaction_date.name: transaction_date
transaction_date.comment: The date that the transaction occurred.

period_first_day.name: period_first_day
period_first_day.comment: First day of the month for the transaction date, useful for monthly reporting and grouping.

period_last_day.name: period_last_day
period_last_day.comment: Last day of the month for the transaction date, useful for month-end reporting and date range filtering.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.comment: Reference to the customer associated with the transaction.

vendor_id.name: vendor_id
vendor_id.comment: Reference to the vendor associated with the transaction.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Monetary amount of the transaction line.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The identifier of the account associated with the transaction line.

class_id.name: class_id
class_id.comment: Reference to the class associated with the transaction line.

department_id.name: department_id
department_id.comment: Reference to the department with the transaction line.

account_number.name: account_number
account_number.comment: User defined number of the account.

account_name.name: account_name
account_name.comment: Name of the account associated with the transaction line.

is_sub_account.name: is_sub_account
is_sub_account.comment: Boolean indicating whether the account is a sub account (true) or a parent account (false).

parent_account_number.name: parent_account_number
parent_account_number.comment: The parent account number. If the account is the parent account then the account number is recorded.

parent_account_name.name: parent_account_name
parent_account_name.comment: The parent account name. If the account is the parent account then the account name is recorded.

account_type.name: account_type
account_type.comment: The type of account associated with the transaction line.

account_sub_type.name: account_sub_type
account_sub_type.comment: Sub type of the account associated with the transaction line.

financial_statement_helper.name: financial_statement_helper
financial_statement_helper.comment: Either 'balance sheet' or 'income statement' and is used to determine that financial statement the account impacts.

account_current_balance.name: account_current_balance
account_current_balance.comment: Total current balance of the account associated with the transaction line.

account_class.name: account_class
account_class.comment: Class of the account associated with the transaction line. Will be one of 'Expense', 'Revenue', 'Asset', 'Liability', and 'Equity'.

transaction_type.name: transaction_type
transaction_type.comment: Identifier of whether the transaction is a debit or credit entry.

transaction_source.name: transaction_source
transaction_source.comment: Field signifying the source of transaction (eg. Bill, Invoice, Deposit, Journal Entry, etc.).

account_transaction_type.name: account_transaction_type
account_transaction_type.comment: Identifier of whether the account is increased by either a debit or credit entry.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Time at which the transaction was created.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Time at which the transaction was last updated.

adjusted_amount.name: adjusted_amount
adjusted_amount.comment: The amount that impacts the account. If the transaction_type = account_transaction_type then the impact will increase, otherwise it will decrease the account.

adjusted_converted_amount.name: adjusted_converted_amount
adjusted_converted_amount.comment: The amount, converted with exchange rates applied if available, that impacts the account. If the transaction_type = account_transaction_type then the impact will increase, otherwise it will decrease the account.

running_balance.name: running_balance
running_balance.comment: The running balance of the account by each individual day.

running_converted_balance.name: running_converted_balance
running_converted_balance.comment: The running balance of the account by each individual day, --converted with exchange rates applied if available.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

account_number.name: account_number
account_number.comment: User defined number of the account. with the account.

account_name.name: account_name
account_name.comment: Name of the account associated with the period balance transactions.

is_sub_account.name: is_sub_account
is_sub_account.comment: Boolean indicating whether the account is a sub account (true) or a parent account (false).

parent_account_number.name: parent_account_number
parent_account_number.comment: The parent account number. If the account is the parent account then the account number is recorded.

parent_account_name.name: parent_account_name
parent_account_name.comment: The parent account name. If the account is the parent account then the account name is recorded.

account_type.name: account_type
account_type.comment: The type of account associated with the period balance transactions.

account_sub_type.name: account_sub_type
account_sub_type.comment: Sub type of the account associated with the period balance transactions.

account_class.name: account_class
account_class.comment: Class of the account associated with the period balance transactions.

class_id.name: class_id
class_id.comment: Reference to the class associated with the period balance transactions.

financial_statement_helper.name: financial_statement_helper
financial_statement_helper.comment: Helper field identifying whether the account impacts the balance sheet or income statement.

period_first_day.name: period_first_day
period_first_day.comment: Timestamp of the first date of the month.

period_last_day.name: period_last_day
period_last_day.comment: Timestamp of the last date of the month.
